categories.title: Documentation Generation
categories.description: Prompts that turn transcripts, codebases, or broad topics into large documentation sets.

categories.title: FAC Form Generation
categories.description: Prompts used by the FAC generation flow to load context, map requirements, and generate code.

categories.title: Workflow Orchestration
categories.description: Prompts that control chat handoff, context loading, checkpointing, and resumable work.

concepts.title: Universal Transcription Documentation Pipeline
concepts.why: Keeps the transcript-to-documentation prompt together in one place because you have multiple copies/variants of the same workflow.
concepts.contains: Markdown prompt variants for converting transcript files into production-grade technical documentation with deep explanations, diagrams, and multi-language examples.
concepts.notes: The two files are near-duplicate variants of the same documentation pipeline prompt.

concepts.title: Kiro Documentation Generator Archive Note
concepts.why: Preserves the richer archived note that wraps a master prompt with frontmatter, rationale, and working context.
concepts.contains: An Obsidian note that embeds a copy-paste-ready Kiro documentation generator prompt plus framing around how to use it.
concepts.notes: This is the archived note form, not the cleaner extracted master prompt.

concepts.title: Kiro Documentation Generator Master Prompt
concepts.why: Keeps the cleaner master-prompt version separate from the archived note so you can reuse it directly.
concepts.contains: A copy-paste-ready master prompt for generating large documentation collections from transcripts using the Kiro methodology.
concepts.notes: This is the streamlined working copy stored under the NodeJs area of your Obsidian vault.

concepts.title: Kiro Comprehensive Documentation Template
concepts.why: Groups the general-purpose exhaustive documentation template and keeps both copies together because they are the same prompt stored in different vaults.
concepts.contains: General Kiro documentation template files focused on exhaustive long-form technical documentation creation.
concepts.notes: These two files are exact duplicates with identical content hashes.

concepts.title: FAC Context Loader
concepts.why: Separates the first-stage FAC context prompt from the downstream prompts because it serves as the bootstrap for the rest of the FAC generation chain.
concepts.contains: The prompt that loads FAC framework knowledge and establishes the system role before requirement analysis or code generation.
concepts.notes: This is the Level 1 prompt in the FAC generation pipeline.

concepts.title: FAC Mapping Generator
concepts.why: Keeps the requirement-to-JSON mapping prompt isolated because it is a distinct stage with a different output contract.
concepts.contains: The prompt that converts natural language form requirements into a structured JSON mapping/specification.
concepts.notes: This is the Level 2 prompt in the FAC generation pipeline.

concepts.title: FAC Code Generator
concepts.why: Keeps the final code-generation prompt separate because it is responsible for producing runnable FAC code from the structured spec.
concepts.contains: The prompt that turns the structured FAC form specification into production-ready Python code.
concepts.notes: This is the Level 3 prompt in the FAC generation pipeline.

concepts.title: Resume Documentation Work
concepts.why: Keeps the resume-chat bootstrap prompt isolated because it is specifically designed to restart a long documentation run safely.
concepts.contains: A restart prompt for continuing `lyik_deployment_templates` documentation work, including progress checks and startup steps.
concepts.notes: This prompt is tuned for resumable documentation runs in Kiro.

concepts.title: Context Ingestion Change Plan
concepts.why: Keeps the context-then-instruction workflow prompt separate because it governs how a chat should process large context dumps before making changes.
concepts.contains: A staged workflow prompt that waits for `STOP-IT`, summarizes context, and only then moves into actual implementation planning.
concepts.notes: Useful when you want the model to absorb context first and avoid premature edits.

concepts.title: Multi-Chat Server Documentation Orchestrator
concepts.why: Keeps the large resumable server-documentation orchestration prompt isolated because it defines state persistence, checkpoints, and per-chat limits.
concepts.contains: A multi-chat orchestration prompt for documenting a large server codebase while persisting progress across limited Kiro chats.
concepts.notes: This is the heavier orchestration prompt focused on summaries, status folders, and resumable runs.
